title: The Highway's Circuit Memory

features.name: traffic ledger
features.desc-on-touch: A chip the size of a shed, pulsing with measurement data. Every vehicle that passed in 1983 is still encoded here—routes, times, durations. The archive has mistaken the ledger for a living thing.

features.name: time circuit
features.desc-on-touch: A vast trace-work carrying electric current that stopped flowing in 1983. Yet the archive reads residual charge—the road still remembers the moment traffic paused to complete a transaction that never finished.

features.name: the attendant's measurement
features.desc-on-touch: A glyph marking the attendant's position—or perhaps their waiting. The attendant is still measuring the time of a transaction sideways through multiple temporal channels.

features.name: vehicle stack
features.desc-on-touch: A stack of vehicles frozen mid-passage: truck, sedan, motorcycle, bus—all layers of one moment in 1983, still attempting to flow forward.

features.name: current trace
features.desc-on-touch: A glowing line that should represent flow but instead represents halt. Where the highway current stopped, the archive's sensors still detect the pressure of motion wanting to continue.

inscription: time flows like traffic: in lanes, through checkpoints, always toward somewhere else, never arriving

file.name: The_Attendant_s_Incomplete_Ledger_1983.csv
file.note: A transaction log from the highway pump. Each entry is a moment; the final entry is still being written, still waiting for the customer who left their transaction running forty-three years ago.

exits.label: back to the station where time waits sideways
further up the highway, toward the horizon that never arrives
off-road, toward the paddocks where highways become suggestions
